body.title: CLOSED: pe_chain failure accumulator: 3+ same-type failures auto-files a design ticket
body.result: infra.pe_failure_log table created; _pe_escalate() upserts failure_type and auto-files T-igor-recurring-* at count>=3
body.description:
pe_chain failures are escalated to CC inbox individually but not accumulated. The same failure type can recur indefinitely without triggering self-improvement. A failure accumulator that tracks patterns and auto-files design tickets closes the observe→improve loop (goal 1.5c path).

Add infra.pe_failure_log table: (failure_type TEXT, count INT, last_seen TIMESTAMPTZ, ticket_filed BOOLEAN). On each pe_chain escalation, upsert the failure_type + increment count. When count >= 3 and ticket_filed=false, call cc_queue.py add to file ticket T-igor-recurring-[failure_type] as status=sprint. Mark ticket_filed=true.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (MEDIUM); new infra.pe_failure_log table (LOW — new table only)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database (Postgres only via db_proxy); theigors/rules/coding (new table creation)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: failure_type taxonomy + upsert + auto-ticket at threshold. Out of scope: root-cause analysis in the filed ticket (CC/Igor job when picking it up); failure_type must be a finite enum, not free text.
**Test plan:** After 3 escalations of type 'old_string_not_found', cc_queue shows T-igor-recurring-old-string-not-found at status=sprint. 4th failure does not duplicate the ticket (ticket_filed=true gate). infra.pe_failure_log row shows count=4.
